text: Hi. This guide walks you through Balance Testing, step by step.
We will cover tests, exercises, linking, participant ratings, and displaying exercises to each user.
Start in WordPress admin. In the left sidebar, click Tests.
This is the Tests area. You will use All Tests, Users Progress, Bulk copy, and Settings.
To create a test, go to Tests, then Add New.
Add the title, instructions, and video. Pick the correct difficulty scale.
Click Publish when the test is ready.
To copy test content into a new exercise, open a published test.
In the Publish box, click Copy To excercise, below Update. This copies content only. It does not link the test.
WordPress opens a new exercise draft. Review it, then click Publish on the exercise.
Open Exercises, then All Exercises, in the left menu.
You can copy the same test many times. Each copy is a separate exercise with no automatic link.
To link a test to an exercise, open that test again in the editor.
In the sidebar, open Linked exercise. Search by title or identifier.
Pick an exercise from the list. Click Update on the test to save the link.
On Tests, All Tests, the Linked exercise column shows which exercise is connected.
For many tests at once, open Tests, Bulk copy to exercises.
Run bulk copy to create exercise drafts from every published test. Then set Linked exercise on each test.
You create participant accounts. Then open Tests, Users Progress.
This list shows every participant. Click VIEW to open one person.
On the website, participants log in to their own account page.
Tasapainotestit is where they take balance tests.
Harjoitukset is where they see exercises you have displayed.
On Tasapainotestit they watch the video and rate difficulty from one to six.
Ratings three and four use the Linked exercise you set on each test.
When their round ends, go back to Users Progress in admin.
On the user detail page, open Exercise assignments.
Suggested exercises appear here. Click Approve on each row, or Approve all suggested.
Use the up and down arrows to reorder. Top row shows first on the site.
You can also search and add an exercise manually.
When the list looks good, click Display exercises.
That button sends exercises to the participant and opens Harjoitukset for them.
Under Tests, Settings, you set exercise countdown days and round email delays.
On Harjoitukset, the participant sees your approved exercises in order.
If they only see a waiting message, you forgot to click Display exercises.
That is the full workflow. Copy content, link exercises, approve, reorder, and display.
Thank you for watching.
